report_name: Frame Security Launches with $50M to Combat AI-Powered Phishing and Deepfakes

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The article discusses the threat of AI-powered phishing, where threat actors generate massive volumes of grammatically perfect and contextually aware phishing emails.

attack_patterns.name: Impersonation
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors use AI to create realistic video deepfakes and audio voice clones of executives to trick employees into making fraudulent wire transfers or revealing credentials.

attack_patterns.name: Generate Deepfakes
attack_patterns.description: Generative AI is used to produce realistic video and audio (voice cloning) for social engineering attacks, such as impersonating executives.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing via Social Engineering LLM
attack_patterns.description: AI chatbots are used by attackers to engage victims in real-time conversations on platforms like SMS, Slack, or Teams to build trust before making a malicious request.

attack_patterns.name: Financial Theft
attack_patterns.description: A potential impact of successful AI-driven social engineering attacks, such as deepfake executive impersonation, is fraudulent wire transfers resulting in financial loss.

identities.name: Frame Security
identities.description: A cybersecurity startup that launched with $50 million in funding to combat AI-powered social engineering attacks through its 'human risk security' platform.

identities.name: Wiz
identities.description: A cloud security company where the founders of Frame Security previously worked.

identities.name: Unit 8200
identities.description: An elite Israeli Intelligence Corps unit where the founders of Frame Security are veterans.
